title: Unix timestamps in wallet DB (not SQLite datetime strings) is correct
description: WalletStore stores created_at/updated_at as INTEGER (Unix timestamps, i64). The struct comment explicitly cites CLAUDE.md §9 (the SQLite datetime string pitfall). This is the right call. A future contributor might suggest using chrono::DateTime or SQLite's datetime() for readability. Do not do that — SQLite datetime string comparison is locale-sensitive and format-sensitive; Unix timestamps are unambiguous integers with trivially correct ordering.
close_reason: Existing code already implements the correct approach
